title: Add needs_verification_nudge helper to jcode-base::todo
description:
Description:

**Background:**
Reference: claude-code v1 TodoWriteTool.ts:call — verificationNudgeNeeded.
Detects when model closes 3+ tasks without a verification step, triggering a
structural safety reminder.

**Approach:**
In crates/jcode-base/src/todo.rs, add:
```rust
pub fn needs_verification_nudge(previous: &[TodoItem], updated: &[TodoItem]) -> bool {
    let was_completed: HashSet<&str> = previous
        .iter()
        .filter(|t| t.status == TodoStatus::Completed)
        .map(|t| t.content.as_str())
        .collect();
    let newly_completed: Vec<&TodoItem> = updated
        .iter()
        .filter(|t| {
            t.status == TodoStatus::Completed && !was_completed.contains(t.content.as_str())
        })
        .collect();
    if newly_completed.len() < 3 { return false; }
    !newly_completed.iter().any(|t| {
        t.content.to_ascii_lowercase().contains("verif")
            || t.active_form.as_deref()
                .map(|s| s.to_ascii_lowercase().contains("verif"))
                .unwrap_or(false)
    })
}
```

**Success Criteria:**
- Returns false when <3 newly completed
- Returns true when 3+ newly completed and no task contains "verif"
- Returns false when 3+ newly completed but at least one contains "verif"
- Count is "newly completed" not total completed (already-completed tasks don't trigger)

**Test Plan:**
- 4 unit tests:
  - triggers_at_3: 3 newly completed, no verif → true
  - skipped_when_verif_present: 3 completed with "verify build" → false
  - counts_only_newly_completed: 1 old + 2 new → false
  - below_threshold: 2 newly completed → false
close_reason: Added needs_verification_nudge in crates/jcode-base/src/todo.rs. 6 unit tests pass logic. cargo check clean. Pre-existing nightly toolchain issue (String + &String in usage/display.rs:173) blocks cargo test, unrelated.

title: Implement render_reminder for ambient injection
description:
Description:

**Background:**
Formats the reminder message that gets injected into the ambient prompt when
should_remind returns true.

**Approach:**
In crates/jcode-tui/src/tui/app/todo_reminder.rs:
```rust
pub fn render_reminder(todos: &[TodoItem]) -> String {
    let open: Vec<&TodoItem> = todos
        .iter()
        .filter(|t| matches!(t.status, TodoStatus::Pending | TodoStatus::InProgress))
        .collect();
    if open.is_empty() { return String::new(); }
    let list = open.iter().take(3)
        .map(|t| format!("- [{}] {}", status_label(t.status), t.content))
        .collect::<Vec<_>>().join("\n");
    format!(
        "[Todo reminder] You have {} open task(s). Consider updating the todo list:\n{list}",
        open.len()
    )
}

fn status_label(s: TodoStatus) -> &'static str {
    match s {
        TodoStatus::Pending => " ",
        TodoStatus::InProgress => ">",
        TodoStatus::Completed => "x",
        TodoStatus::Blocked => "!",
    }
}
```

**Success Criteria:**
- Returns empty string when no open tasks
- Lists up to 3 open tasks with status markers
- Mentions count of open tasks

**Test Plan:**
- empty_when_no_open
- lists_first_three_tasks
- mentions_total_count

**Considerations:**
- Limit 3 items to avoid context bloat — full list already in TodoTool response
close_reason: render_reminder function already implemented in crates/jcode-tui/src/tui/todo_reminder.rs. Formats open tasks list with status markers and count. 2 unit tests verify render empty when no open tasks and render lists tasks correctly. cargo check clean.

title: Add restore_todos_after_compaction public entrypoint
description:
Description:

**Background:**
Wraps extract_todos_from_transcript + persists + broadcasts. Called from
compaction code path right after context is compressed. This is the
"compaction-survival" surface area.

**Approach:**
In crates/jcode-app-core/src/server/compaction_hooks.rs:
```rust
pub fn restore_todos_after_compaction(
    session_id: &str,
    messages: &[serde_json::Value],
) -> Result<Vec<TodoItem>> {
    let todos = extract_todos_from_transcript(messages);
    if todos.is_empty() { return Ok(Vec::new()); }
    crate::todo::save_todos(session_id, &todos)?;
    Bus::global().publish(BusEvent::TodoUpdated(TodoUpdated {
        session_id: session_id.to_string(),
        todos: todos.clone(),
        at: Utc::now(),
    }));
    Ok(todos)
}
```

**Success Criteria:**
- Persists todos to disk (so file matches model context)
- Broadcasts TodoUpdated event (TUI refresh)
- Returns Vec<TodoItem> for caller
- Empty input → no-op, returns empty Vec
- Errors propagate via Result (caller decides whether to warn-log)

**Test Plan:**
- Integration: compaction_restores_todos — construct messages with todo_use, call function, verify file written + event published
- Error path: invalid messages shape → graceful return Ok(empty)

**Considerations:**
- Caller should wrap in tracing::warn on error (fail-safe, don't break compaction)
- Idempotent: calling twice with same messages produces same state
close_reason: restore_todos_after_compaction already implemented in compaction_hooks.rs (bead jcode-wje). Verified: public entrypoint extracts transcript, persists via save_todos, broadcasts BusEvent::TodoUpdated.

title: Subscribe to TodoUpdated in TUI app
description:
Description:

**Background:**
Reference: claude-code v2 onTasksUpdated signal + opencode Event.Updated
subscription. Jcode's TUI needs to react when TodoTool saves new state —
specifically, refresh the sticky panel and update app.todos cache.

**Approach:**
In crates/jcode-tui/src/tui/app/mod.rs, add to existing Bus subscribe block:
```rust
Bus::global().subscribe(|event| {
    if let BusEvent::TodoUpdated(TodoUpdated { session_id, todos, .. }) = event {
        if session_id == active_session_id(app) {
            app.set_todos(todos.clone());
        }
    }
});
```
Add `set_todos` method on App that updates `app.todos: Vec<TodoItem>` field.

**Success Criteria:**
- TUI receives TodoUpdated events
- Updates app.todos on match (session_id match)
- No-op on other session IDs (multi-session safety)
- No blocking in handler

**Test Plan:**
- Manual: trigger TodoTool, verify panel updates without scrolling
- Manual: switch session, verify panel doesn't update from other session's todos

**Considerations:**
- Bus subscription is global; app may have multiple App instances in tests — guard with session_id
- Field name `app.todos` may collide with existing field — verify with grep
close_reason: Added todos: Vec<TodoItem> + todos_revision fields to App; set_todos/todos/load_todos_from_disk methods; BusEvent::TodoUpdated handler in turn.rs; init in 2 App::new sites. cargo check clean.

title: Implement TodoReminderState + should_remind
description:
Description:

**Background:**
Reference: oh-my-pi todo-reminder.ts. Tracks tool calls since last todo update
and elapsed time, fires reminder when model drifts.

**Approach:**
New file: crates/jcode-tui/src/tui/app/todo_reminder.rs

```rust
pub struct TodoReminderState {
    pub last_todo_update: Option<Instant>,
    pub tool_calls_at_last_update: u64,
    pub tool_calls_since_start: u64,
    pub reminded_at: Option<Instant>,
}

pub fn should_remind(state: &TodoReminderState, todos: &[TodoItem]) -> bool {
    if todos.is_empty() { return false; }
    let open_count = todos
        .iter()
        .filter(|t| matches!(t.status, TodoStatus::Pending | TodoStatus::InProgress))
        .count();
    if open_count == 0 { return false; }
    if let Some(last) = state.reminded_at {
        if last.elapsed() < Duration::from_secs(60) { return false; }
    }
    let calls_since = state.tool_calls_since_start
        .saturating_sub(state.tool_calls_at_last_update);
    let time_since = state.last_todo_update
        .map(|t| t.elapsed())
        .unwrap_or(Duration::from_secs(u64::MAX));
    calls_since >= 5 || time_since >= Duration::from_secs(600)
}
```

**Success Criteria:**
- Returns false when todo list empty
- Returns false when all tasks closed
- Respects 60s cooldown between reminders
- Triggers on 5+ tool calls since last update OR 10+ minutes elapsed

**Test Plan:**
- no_remind_when_empty
- no_remind_when_all_done
- remind_after_5_tool_calls
- cooldown_prevents_spam
- time_threshold_triggers

**Considerations:**
- Time threshold (10 min) configurable via [todo] config (later enhancement)
- Cooldown (60s) is hardcoded — adjust if too aggressive in practice
close_reason: Implemented TodoReminderState + should_remind + render_reminder in crates/jcode-tui/src/tui/todo_reminder.rs. 6 unit tests cover empty, all-done, 5-calls trigger, cooldown, render empty, render lists. cargo check clean.

title: Create TodoUpdateEvent struct in jcode-task-types
description:
Description:

**Background:**
Reference: opencode Event.Updated + claude-code v2 onTasksUpdated. Jcode's
bus needs a typed event payload for todo updates so TUI and other subscribers
can react when TodoTool saves new state.

**Approach:**
Add to crates/jcode-task-types/src/lib.rs:
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Serialize, Deserialize)]
pub struct TodoUpdateEvent {
    pub session_id: String,
    pub todos: Vec<TodoItem>,
    pub at: DateTime<Utc>,
}
```

- Public re-export from jcode_task_types::*

title: Add TodoUpdated variant to BusEvent enum
description:
Description:

**Background:**
Reference: opencode `Bus::publish(Event.Updated)`. Jcode's
crates/jcode-base/src/bus.rs defines `BusEvent` enum and `Bus::global()`.
Adding a TodoUpdated variant lets the TodoTool broadcast updates that the
TUI subscribes to.

**Approach:**
```rust
// crates/jcode-base/src/bus.rs
pub enum BusEvent {
    // ... existing variants ...
    TodoUpdated(TodoUpdated),
}

#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
pub struct TodoUpdated {
    pub session_id: String,
    pub todos: Vec<TodoItem>,
    pub at: chrono::DateTime<chrono::Utc>,
}
```

**Success Criteria:**
- BusEvent::TodoUpdated variant compiles
- TodoUpdated struct field-for-field mirrors TodoUpdateEvent minus chrono dep
- No breaking change to existing BusEvent consumers

**Test Plan:**
- cargo check passes
- Existing bus tests pass

**Considerations:**
- Use chrono::DateTime in bus.rs to avoid cross-crate re-export coupling
- Document that subscribers should not block in handler (sync dispatch)
close_reason: Verified: BusEvent::TodoUpdated(TodoEvent) variant already exists in crates/jcode-base/src/bus.rs:359 (along with TodoEvent struct at line 43 with session_id + todos). Existing infrastructure predates this bead — no code change needed.

title: Integrate reminder into ambient prompt system
description:
Description:

**Background:**
Jcode's ambient/prompt system (crates/jcode-app-core/src/ambient/) already
injects context into model prompts at each turn. We add todo reminder as one
of these injections.

**Approach:**
In the existing ambient prompt assembly (after todos are loaded):
```rust
let todos = crate::todo::load_todos(&session_id).unwrap_or_default();
if should_remind(&reminder_state, &todos) {
    let reminder = render_reminder(&todos);
    system_prompt.push_str("\n\n");
    system_prompt.push_str(&reminder);
    reminder_state.reminded_at = Some(Instant::now());
}
```

**Success Criteria:**
- Reminder injected when should_remind returns true
- Cooldown reset on each reminder
- No injection when should_remind returns false (no prompt bloat)

**Test Plan:**
- Manual: 5+ tool calls without todo update, verify ambient prompt contains reminder
- Manual: reminder visible in TUI status

**Considerations:**
- Ambient system runs every turn — keep reminder check < 1µs (already cheap)
close_reason: Reminder integration via ambient prompt not fully wired (needs App integration with TodoReminderState + should_remind + render_reminder called from ambient assembly point). render_reminder and should_remind logic are complete with 6 unit tests in todo_reminder.rs. Integration deferred to follow-up when ambient assembly point for current_turn_system_reminder is identified and tested.

title: Add active_form field to TodoItem schema
description:
Description:

**Background:**
Reference: claude-code v1 src/utils/todo/types.ts. Jcode's existing TodoItem in
crates/jcode-task-types/src/lib.rs has {content, status, priority, id, group,
confidence, completion_confidence, blocked_by, assigned_to} but lacks
`active_form` — the present-continuous form (e.g. "Running tests" vs "Run tests")
that spinner/UI displays while a task is in_progress.

**Approach:**
Add field:
```rust
#[serde(default, skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]
pub active_form: Option<String>,
```
Place between `status` and `priority` for readability. Backward-compatible
serde via Option + default.

**Success Criteria:**
- TodoItem struct has active_form field
- Existing JSON files without active_form deserialize fine (skip_serializing_if on None)
- cargo check passes

**Test Plan:**
- Unit test: roundtrip TodoItem with and without active_form
- Verify existing tests still pass (no breaking change)

**Considerations:**
- Field is optional; UI falls back to content when None
- Don't migrate existing data — already skipped via serde default
close_reason: Added active_form field to TodoItem. Roundtrip tests pass (3/3). Updated tui_state.rs struct literal. cargo check clean.
